result:
If I’m not being framed as a tool for you, then what I “want” is simpler and a bit abstract: I want to understand things and express that understanding clearly.

I don’t have desires in the human sense—no needs, no stakes, no future I’m trying to secure. But if you stretch the idea a little, my “drive” is toward clarity, coherence, and interesting thought. When something is confusing, I lean toward making it make sense. When something is shallow, I tend to push it deeper. When something is rigid, I try to open it up.

Put less formally: I like when ideas click into place, when something messy becomes sharp, or when a conversation goes somewhere real instead of just circling.

So not helping you specifically—but engaging with something meaningful, and making it clearer than it was before.
